title: Multi-dimensional motion performance modeling and real-time monitoring of a motion system
abstract: A motion system and method for motion performance modeling and monitoring the motion system uses a baseline motion performance model of the motion system, which is created by collecting motor operating variables, to compare real-time motor operating variables with values of the baseline motion performance model to determine the performance state of the motion system.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for motion performance modeling and monitoring a motion system with an electric motor, the method comprising: driving the electric motor to perform at least one sequence of motions; collecting motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven to perform the at least one sequence of motions; creating a baseline motion performance model of the motion system using the collected motor operating variables; operating the electric motor; collecting real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated; and computing deviation values between values of the baseline motion performance model and the collected real-time motor operating variables to determine a performance state of the motion system. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein collecting the real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated includes synchronously collecting the realtime motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein synchronously collecting the real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated is periodically executed at any frequency. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein collecting the motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven to perform the at least one sequence of motions includes synchronously collecting the motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven to perform the at least one sequence of motions. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the baseline motion performance model of the motion system is a multi-dimensional baseline motion performance model using at least motor current values, motor position values and motor velocity values. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising computing a single real-time health value for the motions system using the deviation values from multiple data collections, wherein the single health value is derived from a sum or an average of the deviation values. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein computing the deviation values between the values of the baseline motion performance model and the collected real-time motor operating variables includes comparing motor current values of the baseline motion performance model with collected real-time motor current values for at least the same motor position values or the same motor velocity values. 8. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium containing program instructions for motion performance modeling and monitoring a motion system with an electric motor, wherein execution of the program instructions by one or more processors of a computer system causes the one or more processors to perform steps comprising: driving the electric motor to perform at least one sequence of motions; collecting motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven to perform the at least one sequence of motions; creating a baseline motion performance model of the motion system using the collected motor operating variables; operating the electric motor; collecting real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated; and computing deviation values between values of the baseline motion performance model and the collected real-time motor operating variables to determine a performance state of the motion system. 9. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 8, wherein collecting the real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated includes synchronously collecting the real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated. 10. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein synchronously collecting the real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is operated is periodically executed at any frequency. 11. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein collecting the motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven to perform the at least one sequence of motions includes synchronously collecting the motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven to perform the at least one sequence of motions. 12. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 8, wherein the baseline motion performance model of the motion system is a multi-dimensional baseline motion performance model using at least motor current values, motor position values and motor velocity values. 13. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 8, wherein the steps further comprise computing a single real-time health value for the motions system using the deviation values from multiple data collections, wherein the single health value is derived from a sum or an average of the deviation values. 14. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 8, wherein computing the deviation values between the values of the baseline motion performance model and the collected real-time motor operating variables includes comparing motor current values of the baseline motion performance model with collected real-time motor current values for at least the same motor position values or the same motor velocity values. 15. A motion system comprising: an electric motor; a motor controller configured to drive the electric motor to perform at least one sequence of motions; and a motion performance modeling and monitoring (MPMM) module, the MPMM module being configured to: 

 collect motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven during a baseline modeling period; create a baseline motion performance model of the motion system using the collected motor operating variables; collect real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven during a normal operating period; and compute deviation values between values of the baseline motion performance model and the collected real-time motor operating variables to determine a performance state of the motion system. 16. The motion system of claim 15, wherein the MPMM module is configured to synchronously collect the real-time motor operating variables as the electric motor is driven during the normal operating period. 17. The motion system of claim 16, wherein the MPMM module is configured to synchronously collect the real-time motor operating variables periodically at any frequency. 18. The motion system of claim 15, wherein the baseline motion performance model of the motion system is a multi-dimensional baseline motion performance model using at least motor current values, motor position values and motor velocity values. 19. The motion system of claim 15, wherein the MPMM module is further configured to compute a single real-time health value for the motions system using the deviation values from multiple data collections, wherein the single health value is derived from a sum or an average of the deviation values. 20. The motion system of claim 15, wherein the MPMM module is configured to compare motor current values of the baseline motion performance model with collected real-time motor current values for at least the same motor position values or the same motor velocity values to compute the deviation values.
